info.name: Search for Issues Across the Repositories That the User Has Access To

params.name: labels
params.description: Comma-separated list of label names. Fetch only issues that have any of these labels. Non existent labels are discarded.

params.name: milestones
params.description: Comma-separated list of milestone names. Fetch only issues that have any of these milestones. Non existent milestones are discarded.

params.name: since
params.description: Only show issues updated after the given time (RFC 3339 format)

params.name: before
params.description: Only show issues updated before the given time (RFC 3339 format)

params.name: assigned
params.description: Filter issues or pulls assigned to the authenticated user

params.name: created
params.description: Filter issues or pulls created by the authenticated user

params.name: mentioned
params.description: Filter issues or pulls mentioning the authenticated user

params.name: review_requested
params.description: Filter pull requests where the authenticated user's review was requested

params.name: reviewed
params.description: Filter pull requests reviewed by the authenticated user

params.name: created_by
params.description: Only show items which were created by the given user

params.name: team
params.description: Filter by team (requires organization owner parameter)

params.name: page
params.description: Page number of results to return (1-based)

items.docs: Search for Issues Across the Repositories That the User Has Access To

params.name: state
params.description: whether issue is open or closed

params.name: labels
params.description: comma separated list of label names. Fetch only issues that have any of this label names. Non existent labels are discarded.

params.name: type
params.description: filter by type (issues / pulls) if set

params.name: milestones
params.description: comma separated list of milestone names or ids. It uses names and fall back to ids. Fetch only issues that have any of this milestones. Non existent milestones are discarded

params.name: since
params.description: Only show items updated after the given time. This is a timestamp in RFC 3339 format

params.name: before
params.description: Only show items updated before the given time. This is a timestamp in RFC 3339 format

params.name: created_by
params.description: Only show items which were created by the given user

params.name: assigned_by
params.description: Only show items for which the given user is assigned

params.name: mentioned_by
params.description: Only show items in which the given user was mentioned

params.name: page
params.description: page number of results to return (1-based)

params.name: topic
params.description: Limit search to repositories with keyword as topic

params.name: includeDesc
params.description: include search of keyword within repository description

params.name: uid
params.description: search only for repos that the user with the given id owns or contributes to

params.name: priority_owner_id
params.description: repo owner to prioritize in the results

params.name: team_id
params.description: search only for repos that belong to the given team id

params.name: starredBy
params.description: search only for repos that the user with the given id has starred

params.name: private
params.description: include private repositories this user has access to (defaults to true)

params.name: is_private
params.description: show only pubic, private or all repositories (defaults to all)

params.name: template
params.description: include template repositories this user has access to (defaults to true)

params.name: archived
params.description: show only archived, non-archived or all repositories (defaults to all)

params.name: mode
params.description: type of repository to search for. Supported values are "fork", "source", "mirror" and "collaborative"

params.name: exclusive
params.description: if `uid` is given, search only for repos that the user owns

params.name: sort
params.description: sort repos by attribute. Supported values are "alpha", "created", "updated", "size", "git_size", "lfs_size", "stars", "forks" and "id". Default is "alpha"

params.name: order
params.description: sort order, either "asc" (ascending) or "desc" (descending). Default is "asc", ignored if "sort" is not specified.

params.name: page
params.description: page number of results to return (1-based)

info.name: The Extended "contents" API, to Get File Metadata and/or Content, or List a Directory.

params.name: filepath
params.description: path of the dir, file, symlink or submodule in the repo. Swagger requires path parameter to be "required", you can leave it empty or pass a single dot (".") to get the root directory.

params.name: ref
params.description: the name of the commit/branch/tag, default to the repository’s default branch.

params.name: includes
params.description: By default this API's response only contains file's metadata. Use comma-separated "includes" options to retrieve more fields. Option "file_content" will try to retrieve the file content, "lfs_metadata" will try to retrieve LFS metadata, "commit_metadata" will try to retrieve commit metadata, and "commit_message" will try to retrieve commit message.

items.docs: It guarantees that only one of the response fields is set if the request succeeds. Users can pass "includes=file_content" or "includes=lfs_metadata" to retrieve more fields. "includes=file_content" only works for single file, if you need to retrieve file contents in batch, use "file-contents" API after listing the directory.

info.name: Gets the Metadata and Contents (if a File) of an Entry in a Repository, or a List of Entries if a Dir.

params.name: filepath
params.description: path of the dir, file, symlink or submodule in the repo

params.name: ref
params.description: The name of the commit/branch/tag. Default to the repository’s default branch.

items.docs: This API follows GitHub's design, and it is not easy to use. Recommend users to use the "contents-ext" API instead.

params.name: ref
params.description: The name of the commit/branch/tag. Default to the repository’s default branch.

items.docs: Uses automatic pagination based on default page size and max response size and returns the maximum allowed number of files. Files which could not be retrieved are null. Files which are too large are being returned with `encoding == null`, `content == null` and `size > 0`, they can be requested separately by using the `download_url`.
